pattern: Minimum root-to-bottom path cost is found by folding the triangle upward, replacing each entry with itself plus the cheaper of its two children.
hints: From any cell you may step to one of two cells directly below it, so the best cost depends only on the row beneath.
Work from the bottom row upward: the best total reachable from a cell is its own value plus the smaller best of the two cells below.
Copy the last row as running minimums. Moving up each row, set best[j] = triangle[i][j] + min(best[j], best[j+1]); the answer ends up in best[0].

edge_inputs.description: greedy top-down picking the locally smaller child is not optimal
must look ahead two rows to beat a tempting small middle

stress.description: tall random triangle within value bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = max(1, min(size, 200))
    tri = [[rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(i + 1)] for i in range(rows)]
    return [tri]
stress.note: the bottom-up dp is linear per row so even 200 rows finishes instantly
